hero.title: About Us
hero.description1: Eco Operator Kyrgyzstan is the national operator in the field of waste management, established to develop an environmentally sustainable waste recycling and disposal system across the country.
hero.description2: We unite government agencies, the business community, recycling enterprises, and the public to create an effective and transparent waste management ecosystem.
hero.description3: Our activities are aimed at implementing modern recycling standards, supporting environmental infrastructure, and implementing Extended Producer Responsibility mechanisms. We strive to ensure Kyrgyzstan's transition to a "green economy" and reduce the negative impact of waste on the environment.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to key questions about the recycling fee and the waste management system

items.q1: Who must pay the recycling fee?
items.a1: The recycling fee must be paid by manufacturers and importers of goods subject to disposal after loss of consumer properties, including packaging. This applies to legal entities and individual entrepreneurs engaged in the production or import into the territory of the Kyrgyz Republic of goods, the list of which is approved by the Government of KR.
items.q2: How is the recycling fee amount calculated?
items.a2: The recycling fee is calculated by multiplying the weight of goods (in tons) by the recycling fee rate established for the corresponding product group, taking into account recycling standards. Rates are set by the Government of KR and may vary depending on the type and category of goods.
items.q3: Which product groups are subject to the recycling fee?
items.a3: The recycling fee applies to goods from 24 groups: 18 product groups (corrugated and non-corrugated paper/cardboard products, oils, tires and tubes, rubber products, plastic packaging and other products, hollow glass, computers and peripheral equipment, monitors and TV receivers, primary cells and batteries, lead accumulators, rechargeable batteries, lighting equipment, large and small household appliances, refrigeration and ventilation equipment, ICE filters) and 6 packaging groups (halogen-free polymer, halogen-containing polymer, combined, corrugated cardboard, paper and non-corrugated cardboard, glass).
items.q4: How to submit a recycling report?
items.a4: The recycling report is submitted through the personal account of the "SE Eco Operator" system. You need to log in, select the "Recycling Reports" section, and fill out the form specifying the volumes of recycled waste for each product group for the reporting period.
items.q5: Where are the recycling fee funds directed?
items.a5: The collected recycling fee funds are directed to financing the waste collection, sorting and recycling system, developing waste management infrastructure, and stimulating the activities of waste recyclers in the Kyrgyz Republic.
items.a6: EPR is an environmental policy principle according to which the producer's responsibility for a product extends to the entire life cycle of the product, including the post-consumption stage. Manufacturers and importers are obligated to ensure the disposal of waste from the use of their goods or pay the recycling fee.

economics.title: Economic aspects
economics.description: Eco Operator Kyrgyzstan builds a sustainable financial model based on the "polluter pays" principle.
economics.fundsDirectedTo: Recycling fee funds are directed to:

benefits.item1: creation and development of recycling infrastructure

items.desc1: We ensure the collection, distribution and targeted use of funds paid by producers and importers for the subsequent recycling and disposal of their products and packaging.
items.desc2: We finance and support the creation of sorting, recycling, temporary waste storage facilities and logistics solutions for secondary raw materials.
items.desc3: We incentivize recycling enterprises, promote the adoption of new technologies and recycling market support programs.
items.desc4: We raise public awareness about the importance of sorting, recycling and reducing waste.
items.desc5: We ensure transparency in the use of funds, track recycling volumes and publish reports.

businessCalc.pageSubtitle: Calculation of the recycling fee for goods and packaging
businessCalc.bannerDescription: Find out the recycling fee amount based on the weight of goods and packaging. Rates and recycling standards are applied automatically according to the product group.
businessCalc.infoDescription: The recycling fee is calculated based on the weight of goods and product packaging according to rates approved by the Cabinet of Ministers of the Kyrgyz Republic Resolution No. 730 dated December 3, 2024, Appendix 2, and recycling standards approved by the Cabinet of Ministers of the Kyrgyz Republic Resolution No. 322 dated June 19, 2024, Appendix 3.
businessCalc.emptyDescription: Create your first recycling fee calculation
businessCalc.importDateLabel: Date of goods import into KR territory
businessCalc.paymentTermsText:
a) by goods and packaging manufacturers — quarterly, no later than the 15th of the month following the quarter;
b) by goods and packaging importers — upon actual import of goods and packaging into the Kyrgyz Republic, no later than 15 business days from the occurrence of such fact.

publications.title: Publications
publications.subtitle: News, Reports, and Press Releases
publications.bannerDescription: Interactive timeline: discover how many years nature needs for each of the 24 waste categories — from 3 months to 1,000,000 years

decomposition.heroSubtitleText: Every product eventually becomes waste. Discover how many centuries nature needs — and what harm it causes to the ecology of Kyrgyzstan.
decomposition.factorsTitle: What affects the rate of decomposition?
decomposition.infoUVDesc: 5–10 times faster on the surface than deep in a landfill
decomposition.infoOxygenDesc: Without O₂ at depth, decomposition nearly stops
decomposition.infoMoistureDesc: Catalyst for corrosion and environment for microorganisms

washingMachine.name: Washing Machines
washingMachine.description: Body — 50–100 years. Drum plastic — 400–500 years. Rubber — 100+ years. Circuit boards — lead, cadmium, bromine. Without O₂ corrosion ×3–5.
washingMachine.harm: Up to 2 kg of hazardous substances per machine. Microplastics from the tank → soil. Compressor oil contaminates up to 50 m².

refrigerator.name: Refrigerators and Freezers
refrigerator.description: One of the most hazardous household waste items. Freon destroys the ozone layer. Polyurethane foam — 400+ years. PVC — hundreds of years.
refrigerator.harm: One refrigerator — freon destroys ozone over 4 km². PU foam releases isocyanates. Oil → groundwater. PVC when burned → dioxins.

electricStove.name: Electric Stoves
electricStove.description: Steel and cast iron — 50–100 years. Glass-ceramic — 1000+ years. Plastic — 200–400 years. Electronics — lead solder.
electricStove.harm: Glass-ceramic permanently takes up space. Electronics contain lead, tin, brominated flame retardants that accumulate in the food chain.

dishwasher.name: Dishwashers
dishwasher.description: Steel body + polypropylene tank (500 years) + rubber + electronics. Polypropylene is resistant to microorganisms.
dishwasher.harm: Heavy metals from circuit boards leach into soil. Polypropylene → microparticles into groundwater. Rubber releases sulfur and zinc.

television.name: Televisions
television.description: CRT — up to 2 kg of lead. LED/OLED — mercury, indium, lithium. ABS plastic — 400–1000 years. Glass — forever.
television.harm: Lead is a neurotoxin. Mercury evaporates when broken. Indium is toxic to lungs. ABS when burned → styrene (carcinogen).

computer.name: Computers and Laptops
computer.description: Circuit boards: gold, silver, palladium + lead, cadmium, bromine. Li-ion → fires. Screen — mercury.
computer.harm: 1 laptop = 50 m² of poisoned soil. Brominated flame retardants → hormonal disorders. Li-ion → 600°C. 1 ton of circuit boards contains more gold than 1 ton of ore.

monitor.name: Monitors
monitor.description: LCD — liquid crystals + mercury. CRT — 3 kg of leaded glass. ABS — 400+ years.
monitor.harm: Liquid crystals contain biphenyls — POPs that don't decompose and accumulate in organisms. 1 mercury lamp = 30,000 L of contaminated water.

printer.name: Printers and Scanners
printer.description: Body — 400+ years. Toner — iron oxide, carbon black, polymers 5–15 microns, penetrating groundwater.
printer.harm: 1 cartridge = 1000 L of contaminated water. Toner = PM2.5 → lungs. Photoreceptor — arsenic, selenium. 375 million cartridges/year worldwide.

mobilePhone.name: Mobile Phones
mobilePhone.description: Lithium, cobalt, nickel, copper, lead, antimony, indium, tantalum — all in one body. 5.3 billion discarded/year.
mobilePhone.harm: Cobalt is a carcinogen (lung cancer). Antimony → heart and lungs. 5.3 billion/year = 50,000 tons of toxic waste. 1 ton of phones contains 300 g of gold (ore contains 5 g).

electricLamp.name: Electric Lamps
electricLamp.description: CFL: 3–5 mg mercury = 20 m³ of air. LED: arsenic, lead. Glass — 1000+ years.
electricLamp.harm: Broken CFL = mercury MPC ×20. LED — gallium arsenide (arsenic). In KR, lamps are thrown into general waste.

tire.name: Pneumatic Tires
tire.description: Rubber, carbon black, sulfur, zinc, cadmium. Does not biodegrade — breaks down into microparticles. In KR ~15,000 tons/year.
tire.harm: Up to 30 toxic chemicals per tire. When burned — benzo[a]pyrene (class 1 carcinogen). Microparticles — 2nd source of microplastics. Accumulate water → mosquitoes.

accumulator.name: Accumulators
accumulator.description: Hazard class 1. Up to 15 kg lead + 5–7 L sulfuric acid. Lithium-ion — lithium, cobalt, nickel. Body — 400+ years.
accumulator.harm: Acid kills everything within meters. Lead accumulates in the brain — reduces IQ in children. Li-ion → thermal runaway → landfill fires.

battery.name: Batteries
battery.description: Shell — 10–50 years, after which mercury, cadmium, zinc leach into soil for centuries.
battery.harm: 1 battery = 20 m² of soil + 400 L of water. Mercury → Minamata disease (CNS). Cadmium → kidneys. In KR there are no battery recycling points.

constructionPlastic.name: Construction Materials (Plastic)
constructionPlastic.description: PVC pipes, siding, panels. Without UV and O₂ — eternal. Neither bacteria nor fungi can break it down.
constructionPlastic.harm: When burned → dioxins (strongest carcinogens). 57% chlorine → hydrochloric acid. Phthalates — endocrine disruptors.

constructionMetal.name: Construction Materials (Metal)
constructionMetal.description: Regular steel — 50–100 years with moisture. Galvanized — 150–200. Stainless — 500+. Aluminum — up to 500 years.
constructionMetal.harm: Galvanization releases zinc, chrome-plated parts — chromium VI (carcinogen). But metal is infinitely recyclable: 1 ton of recycled steel saves 1.5 tons of ore.

furniture.name: Furniture
furniture.description: Natural wood — 10–15 years. Particleboard — 50–100 years. Foam (polyurethane) — 200+ years. Varnish forms a protective film.
furniture.harm: Particleboard emits formaldehyde — a carcinogen — for up to 10 years. Foam releases cyanides when burned. One particleboard panel pollutes air over an area of up to 30 m².

textile.name: Textile Products
textile.description: Natural fabrics: cotton — 2–5 months, linen — 2 weeks. But 60% of modern clothing is synthetic, which decomposes over 200+ years, breaking into microplastic fibers.
textile.harm: One wash of synthetics releases up to 700,000 microfibers. Dyes contain formaldehyde, chromium. In landfills, textiles emit methane and hydrogen sulfide.

footwear.name: Footwear
footwear.description: Natural leather — 25–40 years. Rubber sole — 50–80 years. Modern sneakers — composite of 30+ materials: EVA foam, polyurethane, polyester, glue.
footwear.harm: Adhesive joints emit formaldehyde and toluene. Rubber releases zinc and sulfur. One pair = up to 14 kg CO₂ in production.

plasticPackaging.name: Plastic Packaging
plasticPackaging.description: PET — 450 years, HDPE — 1000 years, film — 200 years. No light means no photodegradation.
plasticPackaging.harm: Microplastics in the blood of 80% of people. Bisphenol A → infertility. 8 million tons/year into the ocean. By 2050 plastic > fish.

glassPackaging.name: Glass Packaging
glassPackaging.description: SiO₂ — one of the most stable minerals. Artifacts 5000+ years in museums. Doesn't decompose — crumbles.
glassPackaging.harm: Paradox: chemically inert (no toxins), but takes up space FOREVER. Shards are dangerous for animals. Production = 900°C. But 100% infinitely recyclable.

metalPackaging.name: Metal Packaging
metalPackaging.description: Tin plate — 10 years. Aluminum — 80–500 years: oxide film stops corrosion. Foil — 100+ years.
metalPackaging.harm: Al³⁺ ions are toxic to plants and aquatic organisms. 1 can = 170 g CO₂. But recycling saves 95% of energy. 500 years in a landfill vs 60 days of recycling.

paperPackaging.name: Paper Packaging
paperPackaging.description: Cardboard — 3–4 months, newspaper — 1.5 months, office paper — up to 2 years due to bleach. Laminated cardboard — up to 5 years, as film blocks moisture access.
paperPackaging.harm: Printing inks may contain heavy metals. Mass paper decay in landfills is a major source of methane. Laminated packaging cannot be recycled by conventional means.

combinedPackaging.name: Combined Packaging
combinedPackaging.description: Tetra Pak: 75% cardboard + 20% polyethylene + 5% aluminum. Glued layers are indestructible. Standard recycling is impossible.
combinedPackaging.harm: Doesn't decompose in landfills for decades. Aluminum blocks bacteria. In KR there is no equipment for Tetra Pak recycling.

woodPackaging.name: Wooden Packaging
woodPackaging.description: Untreated wood decomposes in 1–3 years thanks to fungi and bacteria. Antiseptic-treated (pallets, crates) — 10–15 years, as treatment kills microorganisms. Deep in landfills without oxygen, even untreated wood is preserved for decades.
woodPackaging.harm: Minimal environmental harm. However, antiseptic treatments (creosote, copper sulfate) release toxic compounds into soil and groundwater. Wood during anaerobic decomposition in landfills emits methane — a greenhouse gas 25 times more potent than CO₂.

instructionCalc.html:
Instructions for Filling Out the Recycling Fee Calculation Form

for producers and importers

Column 1

The sequential number is indicated.

Column 2

The group number of finished goods and their packaging released into circulation in the Kyrgyz Republic is indicated. Numbers and names are provided in accordance with the list approved by Cabinet of Ministers Resolution No. 322 dated 06/19/2024.

Column 3

Product and packaging codes are indicated according to the State Statistical Classifier of Products (SCCP). Filled in by producers. Not filled in by importers.

Column 4

The code for each finished product (packaging) is indicated according to the unified Commodity Nomenclature of Foreign Economic Activity of the Eurasian Economic Union (HS Code). Not filled in by producers.

Column 5

Filled in based on accounting and financial records, as well as invoices and other supporting documents containing information about the total volume of finished products and packaging released into circulation in the Kyrgyz Republic during the reporting period.

Indicators are reflected in tons.

Column 6

Waste recycling standards for product use are indicated, as approved by Cabinet of Ministers of the KR Resolution No. 322 dated June 19, 2024.

Column 7

The total quantity of goods (finished products and packaging) subject to recycling is indicated, taking into account the recycling standard.

Column 7 = Column 5 × Column 6

The result is indicated in tons.

Column 8

The quantity of waste from product use (finished products and packaging) actually transferred or planned for transfer to recycling in the reporting period in accordance with the agreement with a licensed waste recycler is indicated.

Indicators are indicated in tons.

When importing goods and in the absence of actual waste transfer in the reporting period, the value entered in the column is «0».

Actual waste transfer is confirmed by a contract and, if available, acceptance-transfer acts, and is reflected in the report for the period in which such transfer was carried out.

Column 9

The quantity of goods and packaging exported beyond the territory of the Kyrgyz Republic is indicated. Customs documents data is used for filling, and in their absence — documents provided by counterparties in the course of commercial activities.

In the absence of supporting documents on actual export, the value entered in the column is «0».

Indicators are indicated in tons.

Column 10

The difference between the established data (column 7) and actually recycled quantity (column 8), as well as export (column 9) is indicated.

Column 10 = Column 7 − Column 8 − Column 9

The result is indicated in tons.

Column 11

Filled in according to the recycling fee rates established in Appendix 2 to the Cabinet of Ministers of the KR Resolution No. 730 dated December 3, 2024.

Column 12

For producers and importers who do not provide independent recycling:

Column 12 = Column 7 × Column 11

in accordance with clause 13 of Appendix 1 to Resolution No. 730 dated December 3, 2024

For producers and importers who provide independent recycling and/or carried out export:

Column 12 = Column 10 × Column 11

in accordance with clause 15 of Appendix 1 to Resolution No. 730 dated December 3, 2024

Column 13

Filled in based on data on actually accrued and made payments for the reporting period:

  • Producers — based on quarterly payment amounts
  • Importers — based on payment amounts calculated upon actual import of goods and packaging into the KR territory, 15 business days from the date of import

Indicators are specified with precision to two decimal places and are used to determine the presence of overpayment or arrears on the recycling fee.

instructionReport.html:
Instructions for Filling Out the Reporting Form

General Rules

A dash is placed in reporting positions that are not subject to filling. Paper-based reports are signed by the head of the legal entity or a person authorized to act on behalf of the legal entity or an individual entrepreneur or individual, stitched, numbered page by page, and sealed (if available).

Table 1 — Products

Filled in for goods released into circulation in the Kyrgyz Republic, including packaging as a finished product. Information about product packaging is not included in this table.

The table is filled in as follows: first, the number and name of the product group is indicated in accordance with section 1 of the list, and then product items are indicated line by line. Only those product groups for which there is an obligation to ensure waste recycling are included in the table.

Product names are provided according to State Statistical Classifier of Products (goods and services) SC 017-2015 in accordance with the first column of section 2 of the list.

Column 3 — SCCP

Filled in for goods, including packaging as a finished product, released into circulation in the KR by their producers.

Column 4 — HS Code

The name and code for each product are indicated according to the unified Commodity Nomenclature of Foreign Economic Activity of the Eurasian Economic Union (HS Code EAEU), approved by EEC Council Decision No. 80 dated September 14, 2021.

Filled in for goods released into circulation by their importers. A legal entity that is both a producer and an importer fills in columns 3–5.

Column 5 — Units of Measurement

The quantity of goods, product packaging, and waste quantity are indicated for each product in kilograms, rounded to a whole number according to mathematical rounding rules.

Column 6 — Quantity

Filled in based on information obtained during declaration by producers and importers of the quantity of goods and product packaging released into circulation. Information from the declaration for the current reporting year is used.

Column 7 — For Recycling

The quantity of goods released into circulation in the current reporting year for which there is an obligation to ensure compliance with recycling standards based on declaration information is indicated.

Column 8 — Recycled

The quantity of waste from product use collected in the KR and recycled during the reporting period is indicated based on information contained in waste recycling acts.

Column 9 — Above Standard (for the previous period)

The quantity of waste recycled in the previous reporting period above the recycling standard is indicated based on information contained in the report for the previous reporting period.

Column 10 — Total Recycled

The total quantity of waste credited in the reporting period is determined by summing:

Column 10 = Column 8 + Column 9
Column 11 — For Payment

The quantity of waste for which the recycling fee must be paid is determined by subtracting the total quantity of recycled waste from the total quantity of goods subject to recycling.

Column 11 = Column 7 − Column 10

Filled in in case of non-compliance with recycling standards.

Column 12 — Recycling Documents

The following information is provided:

  • When organizing own facilities for recycling — details (number, date) of waste recycling acts
  • When entering into contracts with recyclers — the name of the entity performing recycling, details of contracts and waste recycling acts
  • If compliance with standards was ensured through multiple recyclers — each entity is indicated (as multiple rows for one product)
Column 13 — Notes

Filled in mandatorily if the recycling standard is met through the recycling of interchangeable goods and packaging (of similar purpose and/or similar recycling method).

The column provides explanations allowing to correlate the product subject to recycling with recycled interchangeable goods. In other cases, the column is filled at the discretion of the producer/importer.

Table 2 — Product Packaging

The table includes information about product packaging (excluding packaging as a finished product).

Filled in as follows: first, the product packaging group number is indicated in accordance with section 2 of the list, and then packaging positions are indicated line by line by the material from which the packaging is made.

Columns 14–15 — Packaging Classification

Filled in when it is possible to identify product packaging according to one of the specified classifications (alphabetic and numeric code).

Column 16 — Total Recycled (packaging)

The total quantity of waste from product use (in terms of packaging) credited in the reporting period is determined by summing the quantity of waste recycled in the current period and the quantity of waste recycled in the previous period above the standard.

Column 17 — For Payment (packaging)

The quantity of waste (in terms of packaging) for which the recycling fee must be paid is determined by subtracting the total quantity of recycled waste from the total quantity of packaging subject to recycling.

Filled in in case of non-compliance with recycling standards (in terms of product packaging).

legalBase.title: Legal and Regulatory Framework
legalBase.subtitle: Complete database of legal and regulatory acts in the field of waste management and extended producer responsibility, along with frequently asked questions
legalBase.searchNpa: Search by title, number or description...
legalBase.tryDifferentQuery: Try changing the search query or filter

d1.title: KR Law "On Production and Consumption Waste"
d1.desc: The main law regulating legal relations in the field of production and consumption waste management in the Kyrgyz Republic.

d2.title: KR Law "On Environmental Protection"
d2.desc: Defines the legal basis for environmental protection, ensuring ecological safety, and preventing harmful impact.

d3.title: KR Law "On Environmental Expertise"
d3.desc: Establishes the procedure for conducting environmental expertise of economic activity projects and facilities.

d4.title: KR Law "On the Procedure for Reviewing Citizens' Appeals"
d4.desc: Regulates the procedure for submitting and reviewing citizens' appeals by state bodies. Review period — 30 calendar days.

d5.title: KR Law "On Technical Regulation"
d5.desc: Defines technical safety requirements for products, including packaging and waste.

d6.title: KR Law "On Extended Producer (Importer) Responsibility"
d6.desc: Establishes the mechanism of extended responsibility of producers and importers for the disposal of goods and packaging after loss of consumer properties.

d7.title: KR Government Decree "On Utilization Fee Rates"
d7.desc: Approves utilization fee rates by product groups and recycling standards.

d8.title: KR Government Decree "On the Procedure for Paying the Utilization Fee"
d8.desc: Determines the procedure for calculating, paying and administering the utilization fee.

d9.title: KR Government Decree "On Approval of the Regulation on SE Eco Operator"
d9.desc: Approves the regulation on the state enterprise 'Eco Operator' — the administrator of the EPR system.

d10.title: KR Government Decree "On the Registry of Utilization Fee Payers"
d10.desc: Determines the procedure for maintaining the Registry of utilization fee payers and registration requirements.

d11.title: KR Government Decree "On Digitalization of the EPR System"
d11.desc: Approves the transition plan to the digital platform AIS 'Digital Waste Registry' for automation of EPR processes.

d12.title: MNRETS Order "On Approval of Reporting Forms"
d12.desc: Approves quarterly and annual reporting forms on waste management for enterprises.

d13.title: MNRETS Order "On Licensing Procedure for Waste Recycling Activities"
d13.desc: Regulates the procedure for issuing, suspending and revoking licenses for waste recycling activities.

d14.title: MNRETS Order "On Approval of the Utilization Fee Calculation Methodology"
d14.desc: Establishes the methodology for calculating the utilization fee, calculation formulas and the procedure for determining the taxable volume.

d15.title: MNRETS Order "On the Procedure for Returning the Utilization Fee"
d15.desc: Determines the conditions, procedure and deadlines for returning the utilization fee to recyclers when standards are met.

d16.title: MNRETS Order "On Approval of the KR Waste Classifier"
d16.desc: Approves the waste classifier by types, hazard classes and sources of generation.

d17.title: Sanitary Rules and Norms for Waste Management
d17.desc: Hygienic requirements for the placement and neutralization of production and consumption waste.

d18.title: ISO 14001 Standard — Environmental Management Systems
d18.desc: International standard establishing requirements for environmental management systems for organizations.

d19.title: GOST 30772-2001 — Resource Conservation. Waste Management. Terms and Definitions
d19.desc: Interstate standard defining basic terms and definitions in the field of waste management.

d20.title: EAEU Technical Regulation "On Restriction of Hazardous Substances"
d20.desc: Regulates the restriction of use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.

d21.title: Basel Convention on Transboundary Movements of Hazardous Wastes
d21.desc: International convention on the control of transboundary movements of hazardous wastes and their disposal. KR acceded in 1996.

d22.title: Stockholm Convention on Persistent Organic Pollutants
d22.desc: Convention aimed at eliminating or restricting the production and use of persistent organic pollutants.

d23.title: Aarhus Convention on Access to Environmental Information
d23.desc: Convention on access to information, public participation in decision-making and access to justice in environmental matters.

d24.title: UN Sustainable Development Goals — Goal 12
d24.desc: SDG 12: Responsible Consumption and Production. Ensuring the transition to sustainable consumption and production patterns.

d25.title: EAEU Agreement on Environmental Policy Coordination
d25.desc: Agreement of EAEU member states on coordination of approaches to environmental regulation and waste management.

faq.a1: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is an environmental policy principle under which producers and importers of goods are responsible for the disposal of their products and packaging after loss of consumer properties. In Kyrgyzstan, the EPR mechanism is implemented through payment of the utilization fee to SE 'Eco Operator'.
faq.a2: AIS 'Digital Waste Registry' is an automated information system for accounting and controlling waste management in the Kyrgyz Republic.
faq.q3: Who should register in the system?
faq.a3:
The following must register:
• Producers of goods and packaging in KR
• Importers of goods and packaging to KR
• Organizations engaged in waste recycling
• Landfill operators and collection points
faq.q4: What roles exist in the system?
faq.a4:
The system provides the following roles:
• Payer — producer or importer obligated to pay the utilization fee
• Eco Operator — SE 'Eco Operator', EPR system administrator
• MNRETS Employee — Ministry specialist
• Administrator — system administrator
faq.q5: Who is obligated to pay the utilization fee?
faq.a5: Producers of goods and packaging registered in KR and importers of goods and packaging into KR are obligated to pay the utilization fee.
faq.q6: How is the utilization fee calculated?
faq.a6:
The utilization fee is calculated using the formula:

Amount = Volume (tons) × Recycling Standard (%) × Rate (som/ton)
faq.q7: What are the deadlines for paying the utilization fee?
faq.a7:
The utilization fee is paid quarterly:
• Q1 — by April 25
• Q2 — by July 25
• Q3 — by October 25
• Q4 — by January 25 of the following year
faq.q8: What happens if the utilization fee is not paid on time?
faq.a8: A penalty of 0.1% of the debt amount is charged for each day of delay. The maximum penalty cannot exceed 50% of the principal debt.
faq.q9: Can the paid utilization fee be refunded?
faq.a9:
Yes, a refund is possible when:
• The recycler has met recycling standards
• Erroneous or excessive payment
• Export of goods from KR after fee payment
faq.q10: What recycling standards are in effect?
faq.a10: Recycling standards are established by KR Government decree and vary by product groups.
faq.q11: How to confirm the fact of waste recycling?
faq.a11: To confirm recycling, you need to sign a contract with a licensed recycler and obtain an acceptance-transfer act.
faq.q12: Where to find the list of licensed recyclers?
faq.a12: The registry of licensed recyclers is available in the 'Registries' section of the AIS portal.
faq.q13: What types of waste are subject to recycling under EPR?
faq.a13: Under EPR, packaging, tires, batteries, electronic equipment, and oils are subject to recycling.
faq.q14: How to obtain a waste recycling license?
faq.a14: To obtain a license, submit an application to MNRETS KR with the required documents. Review period — up to 30 working days.
faq.q15: What requirements are imposed on recyclers?
faq.a15: Main requirements: MNRETS license, equipment compliant with environmental standards, reporting in AIS.
faq.q16: Can a license be suspended or revoked?
faq.a16: A license may be suspended or revoked in cases of violation of licensing conditions.
faq.q17: How much does a recycling license cost?
faq.a17: License fee depends on the type of activity: MSW recycling — 5,000 som, industrial waste — 10,000 som, hazardous waste — 20,000 som.
faq.q18: What reports need to be submitted in AIS?
faq.a18: Quarterly calculation of utilization fee, annual declaration, and recycling reports are submitted through AIS.
faq.a19: Quarterly calculation — by the 20th of the month following the quarter, annual declaration — by March 1 of the following year.
faq.q20: What happens for failure to provide reporting?
faq.a20: A warning for the first violation, a fine of 5,000 to 20,000 som for repeated violations.
faq.q21: How to correct an error in a submitted report?
faq.a21: Find the document in your personal account, create a corrective document, and submit it for re-review.
faq.a22: Reporting forms are automatically generated in the AIS personal account.
